{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_bcad260a757647e883fbe85e","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# NOAA Regional Model GRIB2 Archives Research\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tThis document summarizes research into NOAA's public archives for regional model outputs in GRIB2 format, with focus on models using Grid Definition Template (GDT) 3.30 (Lambert-conformal conic) and Data Representation Template (DRT) 5.3 (complex packing).\n6\t\n7\t## Models Using Lambert Conformal Conic Projection (GDT 3.30)\n8\t\n9\t### 1. HRRR (High-Resolution Rapid Refresh)\n10\t\n11\t**Characteristics:**\n12\t- **Resolution:** 3km\n13\t- **Domain:** CONUS and Alaska sectors\n14\t- **Projection:** Lambert Conformal Conic (GDT 3.30)\n15\t- **Update frequency:** Hourly (every hour, 00z-23z)\n16\t- **Archive start:** September 30, 2014 (University of Utah archive)\n17\t\n18\t**File Naming Convention:**\n19\t```\n20\thrrr.tCCz.wrfsfcfFF.grib2\n21\thrrr.tCCz.wrfprsfFF.grib2\n22\thrrr.tCCz.wrfnatfFF.grib2\n23\t```\n24\tWhere:\n25\t- `CC` = cycle runtime (00-23)\n26\t- `FF` = forecast hour (00-48)\n27\t\n28\t**Run Schedule:**\n29\t- **Standard cycles:** All hours (00z-23z), forecast out to FH18\n30\t- **Extended cycles:** 00z, 06z, 12z, 18z run out to FH48\n31\t- **Sub-hourly data:** Available for FH01-FH18 (15, 30, 45 min before the hour)\n32\t\n33\t**Typical File Size:** ~120 MB per GRIB2 file\n34\t\n35\t**Archive URLs:**\n36\t- NOAA NCEP: https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/products/hrrr/\n37\t- University of Utah CHPC Archive: https://mesowest.utah.edu/html/hrrr/\n38\t- AWS Open Data Registry: https://registry.opendata.aws/noaa-hrrr-pds/\n39\t- NOMADS: https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/gribfilter.php?ds=hrrr_2d\n40\t\n41\t**Access Methods:**\n42\t- FTP/HTTPS from NCEP servers\n43\t- AWS S3 (noaa-hrrr-pds bucket)\n44\t- NOMADS with subsetting capabilities\n45\t- University of Utah download tool with metadata (.idx) files\n46\t\n47\t### 2. NAM (North American Mesoscale) - Regional Nests\n48\t\n49\t**Characteristics:**\n50\t- **Resolution:** Varies by domain (1.33km - 6km for regional nests)\n51\t- **Domains:** \n52\t  - CONUS Nest (5km, Grid 227)\n53\t  - Alaska Nest (6km, Grid 198)\n54\t  - Hawaii Nest (3km, Grid 196)\n55\t  - Puerto Rico Nest (3km, Grid 194)\n56\t  - Fire Weather Nest (1.33km CONUS / 1.5km Alaska)\n57\t- **Projection:** Lambert Conformal Conic for regional nests\n58\t- **Update frequency:** 4x daily (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z)\n59\t\n60\t**File Naming Convention:**\n61\t```\n62\tnam.tCCz.conusnest.hiresffh.tm00.grib2\n63\tnam.tCCz.alaskanest.hiresffh.tm00.grib2\n64\tnam.tCCz.hawaiinest.hiresffh.tm00.grib2\n65\tnam.tCCz.priconest.hiresffh.tm00.grib2\n66\tnam.tCCz.firewxnest.hiresffh.tm00.grib2\n67\t```\n68\tWhere:\n69\t- `CC` = cycle runtime (00, 06, 12, 18)\n70\t- `ffh` = forecast hour (00-60)\n71\t\n72\t**Run Schedule:**\n73\t- **Cycle times:** 00z, 06z, 12z, 18z\n74\t- **Forecast hours:** FH00-FH60 for most nests (FH00-FH36 for Fire Weather)\n75\t\n76\t**Archive URLs:**\n77\t- NOAA NCEP: https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/products/nam/\n78\t- NOMADS: https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/\n79\t- UCAR GDEX: https://gdex.ucar.edu/datasets/d609000/\n80\t- READY Archives: https://www.ready.noaa.gov/archives.php\n81\t\n82\t**Access Methods:**\n83\t- FTP/HTTPS from NCEP servers\n84\t- NOMADS with GRIB2 subsetting\n85\t- AWS S3 commands via READY\n86\t\n87\t### 3. RAP (Rapid Refresh)\n88\t\n89\t**Characteristics:**\n90\t- **Resolution:** 13km (CONUS Lambert Conformal)\n91\t- **Domain:** CONUS on Lambert Conformal projection\n92\t- **Projection:** Lambert Conformal Conic (GDT 3.30)\n93\t- **Update frequency:** Hourly (every hour, 00z-23z)\n94\t\n95\t**File Naming Convention:**\n96\t```\n97\trap.tCCz.awp130pgrbfxx.grib2  (13km pressure levels)\n98\trap.tCCz.awp252pgrbfxx.grib2  (20km pressure levels)\n99\trap.tCCz.awp236pgrbfxx.grib2  (40km pressure levels)\n100\t```\n101\tWhere:\n102\t- `CC` = cycle runtime (00-23)\n103\t- `xx` = forecast hour\n104\t\n105\t**Run Schedule:**\n106\t- **Standard cycles:** All hours, forecast out to FH21\n107\t- **Extended cycles:** 03z, 09z, 15z, 21z run out to FH51\n108\t\n109\t**Archive URLs:**\n110\t- NOAA NCEP: https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/products/rap/\n111\t- AWS Open Data Registry: https://registry.opendata.aws/collab/noaa/\n112\t\n113\t**Access Methods:**\n114\t- FTP/HTTPS from NCEP servers\n115\t- AWS S3 (noaa-rap-pds bucket)\n116\t\n117\t## GRIB2 Encoding Specifications\n118\t\n119\t### Grid Definition Template 3.30: Lambert Conformal Conic\n120\t\n121\t**Documentation:**\n122\t- NOAA NCO: https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/docs/grib2/grib2_doc/grib2_temp3-30.shtml\n123\t- NCEP Table 3.1: https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/docs/grib2/grib2_doc/grib2_table3-1.shtml\n124\t\n125\t**Key Parameters:**\n126\t- Grid lengths in units of 10^-3 m at specified latitude (LaD)\n127\t- If Latin1 = Latin2, projection is on a tangent cone\n128\t- LoV is the longitude of the meridian parallel to the y-axis (orientation)\n129\t\n130\t### Data Representation Template 5.3: Complex Packing with Spatial Differencing\n131\t\n132\t**Documentation:**\n133\t- NOAA NCO: https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/docs/grib2/grib2_doc/grib2_temp5-3.shtml\n134\t- ECMWF Code Table 5.0: https://codes.ecmwf.int/grib/format/grib2/ctables/5/0/\n135\t\n136\t**Description:**\n137\t- \"Grid point data - complex packing and spatial differencing\"\n138\t- Divides data fields into groups with similar value ranges\n139\t- Uses spatial differencing to improve compression efficiency\n140\t- Commonly used in meteorological data encoding\n141\t\n142\t## Archive Access Patterns\n143\t\n144\t### HTTPS Access Pattern (NCEP)\n145\t```\n146\thttps://<server>.ncep.noaa.gov/data/<model>/<YYYYMMDD>/<filename>\n147\t```\n148\t\n149\t### NOMADS Subsetting\n150\t- Time subsetting (specific forecast hours)\n151\t- Variable subsetting (specific parameters)\n152\t- Level subsetting (vertical levels)\n153\t- Regional subsetting (lat/lon bounding boxes)\n154\t\n155\t### AWS S3 Access Pattern\n156\t```\n157\ts3://noaa-hrrr-pds/<YYYYMMDD>/hrrr.tCCz.wrfsfcfFF.grib2\n158\t```\n159\t\n160\t## File Size Characteristics\n161\t\n162\t| Model | Typical File Size | Resolution | Forecast Hours |\n163\t|-------|------------------|------------|----------------|\n164\t| HRRR 2D Surface | ~120 MB | 3km | FH00-48 |\n165\t| HRRR 3D Pressure | ~200+ MB | 3km | FH00-48 |\n166\t| NAM CONUS Nest | ~50-100 MB | 5km | FH00-60 |\n167\t| RAP 13km | ~30-80 MB | 13km | FH00-51 |\n168\t\n169\t## Model Run Availability Patterns\n170\t\n171\t### HRRR\n172\t- **Current operational:** V4 (since May 2019)\n173\t- **Archive depth:** University of Utah archive from Sep 2014\n174\t- **Update latency:** ~1 hour after run time\n175\t- **Retention:** AWS 30-day rolling window\n176\t\n177\t### NAM\n178\t- **Current operational:** Multiple nests\n179\t- **Archive depth:** Varies by nest and source\n180\t- **Update latency:** ~1-2 hours after run time\n181\t- **Retention:** Variable (NOMADS has historical archive)\n182\t\n183\t### RAP\n184\t- **Current operational:** RAPv4 (since July 2018)\n185\t- **Archive depth:** Varies by source\n186\t- **Update latency:** ~1 hour after run time\n187\t- **Retention:** AWS 30-day rolling window\n188\t\n189\t## Future Developments\n190\t\n191\t**RRFS (Rapid Refresh Forecast System):**\n192\t- NOAA's next-generation regional NWP model\n193\t- Will eventually replace HRRR, RAP, and NAM\n194\t- Currently in development\n195\t\n196\t## Sources\n197\t\n198\t- [High-Resolution Rapid Refresh (HRRR) - NOAA Regional Model GRIB2 Archives Research\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document summarizes research into NOAA's public archives for regional model outputs in GRIB2 format, with focus on models using Grid Definition Template (GDT) 3.30 (Lambert-conformal conic) and Data Representation Template (DRT) 5.3 (complex packing).\n\n## Models Using Lambert Conformal Conic Projection (GDT 3.30)\n\n### 1. HRRR (High-Resolution Rapid Refresh)\n\n**Characteristics:**\n- **Resolution:** 3km\n- **Domain:** CONUS and Alaska sectors\n- **Projection:** Lambert Conformal Conic (GDT 3.30)\n- **Update frequency:** Hourly (every hour, 00z-23z)\n- **Archive start:** September 30, 2014 (University of Utah archive)\n\n**File Naming Convention:**\n```\nhrrr.tCCz.wrfsfcfFF.grib2\nhrrr.tCCz.wrfprsfFF.grib2\nhrrr.tCCz.wrfnatfFF.grib2\n```\nWhere:\n- `CC` = cycle runtime (00-23)\n- `FF` = forecast hour (00-48)\n\n**Run Schedule:**\n- **Standard cycles:** All hours (00z-23z), forecast out to FH18\n- **Extended cycles:** 00z, 06z, 12z, 18z run out to FH48\n- **Sub-hourly data:** Available for FH01-FH18 (15, 30, 45 min before the hour)\n\n**Typical File Size:** ~120 MB per GRIB2 file\n\n**Archive URLs:**\n- NOAA NCEP: https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/products/hrrr/\n- University of Utah CHPC Archive: https://mesowest.utah.edu/html/hrrr/\n- AWS Open Data Registry: https://registry.opendata.aws/noaa-hrrr-pds/\n- NOMADS: https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/gribfilter.php?ds=hrrr_2d\n\n**Access Methods:**\n- FTP/HTTPS from NCEP servers\n- AWS S3 (noaa-hrrr-pds bucket)\n- NOMADS with subsetting capabilities\n- University of Utah download tool with metadata (.idx) files\n\n### 2. NAM (North American Mesoscale) - Regional Nests\n\n**Characteristics:**\n- **Resolution:** Varies by domain (1.33km - 6km for regional nests)\n- **Domains:** \n  - CONUS Nest (5km, Grid 227)\n  - Alaska Nest (6km, Grid 198)\n  - Hawaii Nest (3km, Grid 196)\n  - Puerto Rico Nest (3km, Grid 194)\n  - Fire Weather Nest (1.33km CONUS / 1.5km Alaska)\n- **Projection:** Lambert Conformal Conic for regional nests\n- **Update frequency:** 4x daily (00z, 06z, 12z, 18z)\n\n**File Naming Convention:**\n```\nnam.tCCz.conusnest.hiresffh.tm00.grib2\nnam.tCCz.alaskanest.hiresffh.tm00.grib2\nnam.tCCz.hawaiinest.hiresffh.tm00.grib2\nnam.tCCz.priconest.hiresffh.tm00.grib2\nnam.tCCz.firewxnest.hiresffh.tm00.grib2\n```\nWhere:\n- `CC` = cycle runtime (00, 06, 12, 18)\n- `ffh` = forecast hour (00-60)\n\n**Run Schedule:**\n- **Cycle times:** 00z, 06z, 12z, 18z\n- **Forecast hours:** FH00-FH60 for most nests (FH00-FH36 for Fire Weather)\n\n**Archive URLs:**\n- NOAA NCEP: https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/products/nam/\n- NOMADS: https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/\n- UCAR GDEX: https://gdex.ucar.edu/datasets/d609000/\n- READY Archives: https://www.ready.noaa.gov/archives.php\n\n**Access Methods:**\n- FTP/HTTPS from NCEP servers\n- NOMADS with GRIB2 subsetting\n- AWS S3 commands via READY\n\n### 3. RAP (Rapid Refresh)\n\n**Characteristics:**\n- **Resolution:** 13km (CONUS Lambert Conformal)\n- **Domain:** CONUS on Lambert Conformal projection\n- **Projection:** Lambert Conformal Conic (GDT 3.30)\n- **Update frequency:** Hourly (every hour, 00z-23z)\n\n**File Naming Convention:**\n```\nrap.tCCz.awp130pgrbfxx.grib2  (13km pressure levels)\nrap.tCCz.awp252pgrbfxx.grib2  (20km pressure levels)\nrap.tCCz.awp236pgrbfxx.grib2  (40km pressure levels)\n```\nWhere:\n- `CC` = cycle runtime (00-23)\n- `xx` = forecast hour\n\n**Run Schedule:**\n- **Standard cycles:** All hours, forecast out to FH21\n- **Extended cycles:** 03z, 09z, 15z, 21z run out to FH51\n\n**Archive URLs:**\n- NOAA NCEP: https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/products/rap/\n- AWS Open Data Registry: https://registry.opendata.aws/collab/noaa/\n\n**Access Methods:**\n- FTP/HTTPS from NCEP servers\n- AWS S3 (noaa-rap-pds bucket)\n\n## GRIB2 Encoding Specifications\n\n### Grid Definition Template 3.30: Lambert Conformal Conic\n\n**Documentation:**\n- NOAA NCO: https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/docs/grib2/grib2_doc/grib2_temp3-30.shtml\n- NCEP Table 3.1: https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/docs/grib2/grib2_doc/grib2_table3-1.shtml\n\n**Key Parameters:**\n- Grid lengths in units of 10^-3 m at specified latitude (LaD)\n- If Latin1 = Latin2, projection is on a tangent cone\n- LoV is the longitude of the meridian parallel to the y-axis (orientation)\n\n### Data Representation Template 5.3: Complex Packing with Spatial Differencing\n\n**Documentation:**\n- NOAA NCO: https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/docs/grib2/grib2_doc/grib2_temp5-3.shtml\n- ECMWF Code Table 5.0: https://codes.ecmwf.int/grib/format/grib2/ctables/5/0/\n\n**Description:**\n- \"Grid point data - complex packing and spatial differencing\"\n- Divides data fields into groups with similar value ranges\n- Uses spatial differencing to improve compression efficiency\n- Commonly used in meteorological data encoding\n\n## Archive Access Patterns\n\n### HTTPS Access Pattern (NCEP)\n```\nhttps://<server>.ncep.noaa.gov/data/<model>/<YYYYMMDD>/<filename>\n```\n\n### NOMADS Subsetting\n- Time subsetting (specific forecast hours)\n- Variable subsetting (specific parameters)\n- Level subsetting (vertical levels)\n- Regional subsetting (lat/lon bounding boxes)\n\n### AWS S3 Access Pattern\n```\ns3://noaa-hrrr-pds/<YYYYMMDD>/hrrr.tCCz.wrfsfcfFF.grib2\n```\n\n## File Size Characteristics\n\n| Model | Typical File Size | Resolution | Forecast Hours |\n|-------|------------------|------------|----------------|\n| HRRR 2D Surface | ~120 MB | 3km | FH00-48 |\n| HRRR 3D Pressure | ~200+ MB | 3km | FH00-48 |\n| NAM CONUS Nest | ~50-100 MB | 5km | FH00-60 |\n| RAP 13km | ~30-80 MB | 13km | FH00-51 |\n\n## Model Run Availability Patterns\n\n### HRRR\n- **Current operational:** V4 (since May 2019)\n- **Archive depth:** University of Utah archive from Sep 2014\n- **Update latency:** ~1 hour after run time\n- **Retention:** AWS 30-day rolling window\n\n### NAM\n- **Current operational:** Multiple nests\n- **Archive depth:** Varies by nest and source\n- **Update latency:** ~1-2 hours after run time\n- **Retention:** Variable (NOMADS has historical archive)\n\n### RAP\n- **Current operational:** RAPv4 (since July 2018)\n- **Archive depth:** Varies by source\n- **Update latency:** ~1 hour after run time\n- **Retention:** AWS 30-day rolling window\n\n## Future Developments\n\n**RRFS (Rapid Refresh Forecast System):**\n- NOAA's next-generation regional NWP model\n- Will eventually replace HRRR, RAP, and NAM\n- Currently in development\n\n## Sources\n\n- [High-Resolution Rapid Refresh (HRRR) - NOAA](https://rapidrefresh.noaa.gov/hrrr/)\n- [NCEP Data Products HRRR](https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/products/hrrr/)\n- [NCEP Data Products NAM](https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/products/nam/)\n- [NCEP Data Products RAP](https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/products/rap/)\n- [GRIB2 Table 3.1 - Grid Definition Template Number](https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/docs/grib2/grib2_doc/grib2_table3-1.shtml)\n- [GRIB2 Table 5.0 - Data Representation Template Number](https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/docs/grib2/grib2_doc/grib2_table5-0.shtml)\n- [GRIB2 Template 3.30 - Lambert Conformal](https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/docs/grib2/grib2_doc/grib2_temp3-30.shtml)\n- [GRIB2 Template 5.3 - Complex Packing](https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/docs/grib2/grib2_doc/grib2_temp5-3.shtml)\n- [Registry of Open Data on AWS - NOAA](https://registry.opendata.aws/collab/noaa/)\n- [NOAA HRRR on AWS](https://registry.opendata.aws/noaa-hrrr-pds/)\n- [NOMADS Data Server](https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/)\n- [READY Gridded Data Archives](https://www.ready.noaa.gov/archives.php)\n- [University of Utah HRRR Archive](https://mesowest.utah.edu/html/hrrr/)\n- [Brian Blaylock's HRRR FAQ](https://home.chpc.utah.edu/~u0553130/Brian_Blaylock/hrrr_FAQ.html)\n- [RAP Model Metadata - NOAA NCEI](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/metadata/landing-page/bin/iso?id=gov.noaa.ncdc:C00690)\n","numLines":213,"startLine":1,"totalLines":213}}}

This document catalogs the primary hosting platforms, their base URLs, and access requirements.\n9\t\n10\t---\n11\t\n12\t## 1. NOMADS (NOAA Operational Model Archive and Distribution System)\n13\t\n14\t**Description:** The primary portal for accessing NOAA operational model data with near-real-time access.\n15\t\n16\t**Base URLs:**\n17\t- Main server: `https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/`\n18\t- Data access: `https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/data/`\n19\t\n20\t**Documentation:**\n21\t- Help page: https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/info.php?page=help\n22\t- Fast download guide: https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/info.php?page=fastdownload\n23\t\n24\t**Models Available:**\n25\t- GFS (Global Forecast System)\n26\t- GDAS (Global Data Assimilation System)\n27\t- HRRR (High-Resolution Rapid Refresh) - hourly and sub-hourly\n28\t- HRRR Alaska\n29\t- NAM (North American Mesoscale)\n30\t- HWRF (Hurricane Weather Research and Forecasting)\n31\t- HMON (Hurricane MONitoring)\n32\t\n33\t**Archive URL Patterns:**\n34\t```\n35\t# GFS example\n36\thttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/data/nccf/com/gfs/prod/gfs.YYYYMMDDHH/\n37\t\n38\t# HRRR example\n39\thttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/hrrr/prod/\n40\t\n41\t# Direct GFS data access\n42\thttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/gfs/\n43\t```\n44\t\n45\t**Access Requirements:**\n46\t- No authentication required (public data)\n47\t- HTTP/HTTPS access\n48\t- Supports random access using index files for GRIB downloads\n49\t\n50\t**Limitations:**\n51\t- Limited historical data retention (primarily operational data)\n52\t- For older historical data, use NCEI archives\n53\t\n54\t---\n55\t\n56\t## 2. NCEI (National Centers for Environmental Information)\n57\t\n58\t**Description:** NOAA's official archive for long-term storage of weather and climate data.\n59\t\n60\t**Base URLs:**\n61\t- Main portal: `https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/`\n62\t- Data access search: `https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/search/index`\n63\t- Archive services: `https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/archive`\n64\t- AIRS (Archive Information Request System): `https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/has/HAS.DsSelect`\n65\t\n66\t**Models Available:**\n67\t- Historical weather model output\n68\t- Climate reanalysis datasets\n69\t- Specialized datasets\n70\t- GFS GRIB-2 specific access: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/has/HAS.FileAppRouter?datasetname=GFSGRB24&subqueryby=STATION&applname=&outdest=FILE\n71\t\n72\t**Access Requirements:**\n73\t- Free account registration required for some services\n74\t- AIRS system requires login for order requests\n75\t- Public data search available without authentication\n76\t\n77\t**Data Format:**\n78\t- Native archive formats (including GRIB2)\n79\t- Quality-controlled datasets\n80\t\n81\t---\n82\t\n83\t## 3. NOAA Big Data Program (NODD) - Cloud Platforms\n84\t\n85\t### 3.1 AWS (Amazon Web Services)\n86\t\n87\t**Registry Page:** https://registry.opendata.aws/collab/noaa/\n88\t\n89\t**Available buckets\n108\t- AWS account required (free to access data)\n109\t- Standard AWS egress fees may apply\n110\t- Trailing 30-day window for operational data\n111\t\n112\t**Program Overview:** https://ncics.org/data/noaa-big-data-project/\n113\t\n114\t### 3.2 Google Cloud Platform\n115\t\n116\t**Program Announcement:** https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/data-analytics/weather-climate-big-data-from-noaa-now-in-cloud\n117\t\n118\t**Available Models:**\n119\t- GFS, HRRR, RAP, NAM (same as AWS, through NOAA NODD program)\n120\t\n121\t**Access Requirements:**\n122\t- Public cloud storage buckets\n123\t- GCP account required (free to access data)\n124\t- Standard egress/processing fees may apply\n125\t\n126\t### 3.3 Microsoft Planetary Computer\n127\t\n128\t**Dataset Page:** https://planetarycomputer.microsoft.com/dataset/storage/noaa-hrrr\n129\t\n130\t**Available Models:**\n131\t- HRRR (High-Resolution Rapid Refresh) at 3km resolution\n132\t- Hourly updates, cloud-resolving atmospheric model\n133\t\n134\t**Access Requirements:**\n135\t- No authentication required (public data)\n136\t- Programmatic access via Microsoft's Planetary Computer API\n137\t- STAC (SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog) metadata for discovery\n138\t\n139\t**Use Case:** Optimized for HRRR-specific access with Microsoft's geospatial tools\n140\t\n141\t---\n142\t\n143\t## 4. NOAA READY Gridded Data Archives\n144\t\n145\t**Description:** NOAA ARL's archive system where NCEP model output is reprocessed and stored.\n146\t\n147\t**Base URL:** https://www.ready.noaa.gov/archives.php\n148\t\n149\t**Data Processing:**\n150\t- NCEP model output in GRIB format\n151\t- Reprocessed with 1-byte packing algorithm\n152\t\n153\t**Access Requirements:**\n154\t- No authentication required\n155\t- HTTP/HTTPS access\n156\t- AWS CLI instructions provided for bulk downloads\n157\t\n158\t---\n159\t\n160\t## 5. See: https://gribstream.com/blog/noaa-replaces-cdas-with-core-march-2026\n212\t\n213\t4. **Archive vs. Operational:** NOMADS focuses on operational data (recent), while NCEI provides long-term archival storage.\n214\t\n215\t---\n216\t\n217\t## Sources\n218\t\n219\t- [NOMADS at ncep.noaa.gov](https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/)\n220\t- [NCEI Data Access](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/search/index)\n221\t- [Registry of Open Data on AWS - NOAA](https://registry.opendata.aws/collab/noaa/)\n222\t- [NOAA GFS AWS Marketplace](https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/pp/prodview-hok7o2o24ktfi)\n223\t- [Weather, climate big data from NOAA now in cloud - Google Cloud](https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/data-analytics/weather-climate-big-data-from-noaa-now-in-cloud)\n224\t- [NOAA READY Gridded Data Archives](https://www.ready.noaa.gov/archives.php)\n225\t- [NCEP Products Inventory - GFS](https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/products/gfs/)\n226\t- [Climate Prediction Center - Fast Downloading GRIB](https://www.cpc.ncep.noaa.gov/products/tools/fast_downloading_grib.html)\n227\t- [UCAR GDE - Historical GFS Archive](https://gdex.ucar.edu/datasets/d084001/)\n228\t- [Herbie Documentation](https://herbie.readthedocs.io/)\n229\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"580db4dd-be67-4efc-a81e-3d23e47086f7","uuid":"21f5c8a7-c014-41a4-86f1-28080e7c4a8d","timestamp":"2026-07-09T03:35:58.822Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/gribtract/notes/bf-2p57.md","content":"# NOAA GRIB2 Archive Hosting Platforms\n\n**Bead ID:** bf-2p57  \n**Date:** 2026-07-03\n\n## Overview\n\nNOAA provides public access to weather model GRIB2 data through multiple platforms and archives. This document catalogs the primary hosting platforms, their base URLs, and access requirements.\n\n---\n\n## 1. NOMADS (NOAA Operational Model Archive and Distribution System)\n\n**Description:** The primary portal for accessing NOAA operational model data with near-real-time access.\n\n**Base URLs:**\n- Main server: `https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/`\n- Data access: `https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/data/`\n\n**Documentation:**\n- Help page: https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/info.php?page=help\n- Fast download guide: https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/info.php?page=fastdownload\n\n**Models Available:**\n- GFS (Global Forecast System)\n- GDAS (Global Data Assimilation System)\n- HRRR (High-Resolution Rapid Refresh) - hourly and sub-hourly\n- HRRR Alaska\n- NAM (North American Mesoscale)\n- HWRF (Hurricane Weather Research and Forecasting)\n- HMON (Hurricane MONitoring)\n\n**Archive URL Patterns:**\n```\n# GFS example\nhttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/data/nccf/com/gfs/prod/gfs.YYYYMMDDHH/\n\n# HRRR example\nhttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/hrrr/prod/\n\n# Direct GFS data access\nhttps://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/pub/data/nccf/com/gfs/\n```\n\n**Access Requirements:**\n- No authentication required (public data)\n- HTTP/HTTPS access\n- Supports random access using index files for GRIB downloads\n\n**Limitations:**\n- Limited historical data retention (primarily operational data)\n- For older historical data, use NCEI archives\n\n---\n\n## 2. NCEI (National Centers for Environmental Information)\n\n**Description:** NOAA's official archive for long-term storage of weather and climate data.\n\n**Base URLs:**\n- Main portal: `https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/`\n- Data access search: `https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/search/index`\n- Archive services: `https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/archive`\n- AIRS (Archive Information Request System): `https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/has/HAS.DsSelect`\n\n**Models Available:**\n- Historical weather model output\n- Climate reanalysis datasets\n- Specialized datasets\n- GFS GRIB-2 specific access: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/has/HAS.FileAppRouter?datasetname=GFSGRB24&subqueryby=STATION&applname=&outdest=FILE\n\n**Access Requirements:**\n- Free account registration required for some services\n- AIRS system requires login for order requests\n- Public data search available without authentication\n\n**Data Format:**\n- Native archive formats (including GRIB2)\n- Quality-controlled datasets\n\n---\n\n## 3. NOAA Big Data Program (NODD) - Cloud Platforms\n\n### 3.1 AWS (Amazon Web Services)\n\n**Registry Page:** https://registry.opendata.aws/collab/noaa/\n\n**Available Models:**\n- GFS (Global Forecast System)\n- HRRR (High-Resolution Rapid Refresh)\n- NAM (North American Mesoscale)\n- RAP (Rapid Refresh)\n\n**AWS Marketplace Listings:**\n- GFS: https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/pp/prodview-hok7o2o24ktfi\n- GFS netCDF: https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/pp/prodview-u4bxtndbzw4hk\n\n**Historical Archive:**\n- UCAR GDE: https://gdex.ucar.edu/datasets/d084001/\n- GFS 0.25° global forecast grids (2021 to present)\n\n**Quickstart Resources:**\n- AWS GFS Quickstart Notebook: https://github.com/aws-samples/aws-opendata-samples/blob/main/notebooks/noaa-gfs/noaa_gfs_quickstart.ipynb\n\n**Access Requirements:**\n- No authentication for public buckets\n- AWS account required (free to access data)\n- Standard AWS egress fees may apply\n- Trailing 30-day window for operational data\n\n**Program Overview:** https://ncics.org/data/noaa-big-data-project/\n\n### 3.2 Google Cloud Platform\n\n**Program Announcement:** https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/data-analytics/weather-climate-big-data-from-noaa-now-in-cloud\n\n**Available Models:**\n- GFS, HRRR, RAP, NAM (same as AWS, through NOAA NODD program)\n\n**Access Requirements:**\n- Public cloud storage buckets\n- GCP account required (free to access data)\n- Standard egress/processing fees may apply\n\n### 3.3 Microsoft Planetary Computer\n\n**Dataset Page:** https://planetarycomputer.microsoft.com/dataset/storage/noaa-hrrr\n\n**Available Models:**\n- HRRR (High-Resolution Rapid Refresh) at 3km resolution\n- Hourly updates, cloud-resolving atmospheric model\n\n**Access Requirements:**\n- No authentication required (public data)\n- Programmatic access via Microsoft's Planetary Computer API\n- STAC (SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog) metadata for discovery\n\n**Use Case:** Optimized for HRRR-specific access with Microsoft's geospatial tools\n\n---\n\n## 4. NOAA READY Gridded Data Archives\n\n**Description:** NOAA ARL's archive system where NCEP model output is reprocessed and stored.\n\n**Base URL:** https://www.ready.noaa.gov/archives.php\n\n**Data Processing:**\n- NCEP model output in GRIB format\n- Reprocessed with 1-byte packing algorithm\n\n**Access Requirements:**\n- No authentication required\n- HTTP/HTTPS access\n- AWS CLI instructions provided for bulk downloads\n\n---\n\n## 5. NCEP Products Inventory\n\n**Description:** Official inventory of NCEP data products.\n\n**Base URL:** https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/products/gfs/\n\n**Products Available:**\n- Global Forecast System (GFS) Model\n- Global Data Assimilation System (GDAS) Model  \n- GFS Wave Model\n\n**Access Requirements:**\n- No authentication required\n- HTTP/HTTPS access\n\n---\n\n## 6. Additional Tools and Resources\n\n**Herbie Package:** Python package for accessing NOMADS data  \nDocumentation: https://herbie.readthedocs.io/\n\n**ArchiveGribGrab:** R package for accessing archived NOMADS model data\n\n**Grib Downloader:** GitHub tool for downloading daily GRIB data from NOMADS\n\n**Climate Prediction Center (CPC) Tools:**\n- GRIB download utilities: https://www.cpc.ncep.noaa.gov/products/tools/fast_downloading_grib.html\n- get_grib.pl script for filtered downloads\n\n---\n\n## Summary Table\n\n| Platform | Base URL | Auth Required | Data Retention | Primary Models |\n|----------|----------|---------------|----------------|----------------|\n| NOMADS | `https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/` | No | Operational (~30 days) | GFS, GDAS, HRRR, NAM, HWRF, HMON |\n| NCEI | `https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/` | Yes (AIRS) | Long-term archive | All models |\n| AWS Open Data | AWS Registry | No | 30-day rolling + historical | GFS, HRRR, NAM, RAP |\n| Google Cloud | GCP buckets | No | Varies by model | GFS, HRRR, NAM, RAP |\n| Microsoft Planetary Computer | https://planetarycomputer.microsoft.com/ | No | HRRR archive | HRRR (3km) |\n| READY | `https://www.ready.noaa.gov/` | No | Archive | NCEP output |\n\n---\n\n## Notes\n\n1. **No Authentication Required for Most Public Data:** All platforms listed provide free public access without authentication requirements, with the exception of NCEI's AIRS system which requires account registration for ordering.\n\n2. **Format:** All platforms serve data in GRIB2 format (some also offer netCDF conversions).\n\n3. **Transition Note:** NOAA is transitioning from CDAS to CORe (Cloud Reanalysis) in March 2026. See: https://gribstream.com/blog/noaa-replaces-cdas-with-core-march-2026\n\n4. **Archive vs. Operational:** NOMADS focuses on operational data (recent), while NCEI provides long-term archival storage.\n\n---\n\n## Sources\n\n- [NOMADS at ncep.noaa.gov](https://nomads.ncep.noaa.gov/)\n- [NCEI Data Access](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/search/index)\n- [Registry of Open Data on AWS - NOAA](https://registry.opendata.aws/collab/noaa/)\n- [NOAA GFS AWS Marketplace](https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/pp/prodview-hok7o2o24ktfi)\n- [Weather, climate big data from NOAA now in cloud - Google Cloud](https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/data-analytics/weather-climate-big-data-from-noaa-now-in-cloud)\n- [NOAA READY Gridded Data Archives](https://www.ready.noaa.gov/archives.php)\n- [NCEP Products Inventory - GFS](https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/products/gfs/)\n- [Climate Prediction Center - Fast Downloading GRIB](https://www.cpc.ncep.noaa.gov/products/tools/fast_downloading_grib.html)\n- [UCAR GDE - Historical GFS Archive](https://gdex.ucar.edu/datasets/d084001/)\n- [Herbie Documentation](https://herbie.readthedocs.io/)\n","numLines":229,"startLine":1,"totalLines":229}}}
